# AgentOS

> AgentOS is a repo-local operating system for AI coding agents. You install one
> `agent-os/` kernel into any project and every agent that works there — Claude Code,
> OpenAI Codex, or any AGENTS.md-aware runtime — inherits the same enforced discipline:
> a hook-enforced per-turn audit trail, review gates against sycophancy and unsupported
> claims, task contracts, route keeping, and a durable memory system. The rules live
> once in the kernel; thin adapters project them into each runtime.

AgentOS treats agent reliability as an operating-system problem, not a prompting
problem. `AGENTS.md` and `CLAUDE.md` are advisory instructions an agent may ignore;
AgentOS converts the highest-value invariants into mechanism. The signature mechanism is
a Stop hook that blocks a turn from ending until the agent has appended a well-formed,
uniquely-numbered entry to `agent-os/state/audit-log.md`. It is runtime-agnostic,
dependency-free (Python 3 only), non-destructive on install, and licensed Apache-2.0.

Key facts:
- Kernel vs. adapters: `agent-os/` is the single canonical source of rules; `AGENTS.md`,
  `CLAUDE.md`, `.claude/`, `.codex/`, and `.agents/` are adapters that point back to it.
- Enforcement is layered: hooks mechanically enforce the audit trail's existence/format,
  the kernel's structure (`aos-lint.py`), and an edit guard on the enforcement layer.
  The review gates are routed by the rules card, not hook-forced.
- Honest scope: the hooks enforce structure, format, and numbering — not truthfulness or
  quality. `aos-lint` proves structure only.
- Supported runtimes: Claude Code and OpenAI Codex get full hook enforcement; other
  AGENTS.md-aware agents inherit the rules card, gates, and workflows.

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/zuoxu3310/AGENTOS/blob/main/README.md): full overview, install, architecture, FAQ.
- [Quickstart](https://github.com/zuoxu3310/AGENTOS/blob/main/docs/QUICKSTART.md): install and validate in three commands.
- [Architecture](https://github.com/zuoxu3310/AGENTOS/blob/main/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): the kernel/adapter/hook layers in depth.
- [Installer skill manifest](https://github.com/zuoxu3310/AGENTOS/blob/main/SKILL.md): how the installer skill is triggered and what it does.

## Concepts

- [The kernel](https://github.com/zuoxu3310/AGENTOS/tree/main/assets/agentos-template/agent-os): rules, routing, review gates, workflows, state, memory, tools.
- [Review gates](https://github.com/zuoxu3310/AGENTOS/tree/main/assets/agentos-template/agent-os/review): task-contract, anti-sycophancy, minimal-code, evidence-to-claim, reasoning-causality, intent-causal, route-keeper/promotion, prompt-craft, per-turn-audit, completion.
- [Workflows](https://github.com/zuoxu3310/AGENTOS/tree/main/assets/agentos-template/agent-os/workflows): agent-execution-lifecycle, dynamic-workflow, fusion-workflow.
- [Enforcement hooks](https://github.com/zuoxu3310/AGENTOS/tree/main/assets/agentos-template/.claude/hooks): SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, Stop, PreToolUse, PostToolUse.
